design_philosophy.core_loop: Compete in motorcycle races across California courses while engaging in real-time combat with opponents; earn money to upgrade bikes; progress through difficulty tiers by winning races
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing speed/positioning with stamina management during fights; risk of ejection from bike via crash or stamina depletion; financial consequences of damage/fines
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Aggressive motorcycle racer who fights opponents mid-race using situational combat (punches, kicks, backhand, stolen weapons)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unconventional hybrid of racing and brawler combat; motorcycle racing depicted as inherently violent and chaotic; combat is mandatory, not optional; financial attrition creates stakes beyond typical racing metrics

difficulty.continues_system: Game ends prematurely if player lacks funds to cover repair bills or police fines; password system allows session continuation
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based progression system; must win all 5 races on a level to advance; advancement is linear across 5 difficulty tiers
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Bike purchasing system allows meta-progression through economic strategy; choice of bike weight/speed/handling trades off maneuverability vs performance; no explicit difficulty selection

progression.upgrade_system: Between-race bike shop; player accumulates $1,000 starting cash and race winnings to purchase bikes with different weight, speed, and steering characteristics
progression.unlock_mechanism: Must finish 4th or higher in each race to progress; must win all 5 races on a level to unlock next difficulty tier; 25 total races across 5 levels
replayability_hooks: Different bike configurations create varied handling experiments
Multiplayer head-to-head mode for intermittent competition
Financial attrition encourages optimization of racing performance
Variety of California settings (Grass Valley, Sierra Nevada, Napa Valley, Pacific Coast Highway)

world_building_techniques: California racing circuit setting establishes cultural context
Procedural opponents create impression of living racing community
Motor officers represent law enforcement authority
Environmental hazards (cows, deer, cars, trees) suggest semi-realistic open road racing

faction_indicators: Competing racers as rivals (15 opponents per race)
danger_signals: Stamina depletion forces ejection and vulnerability
Bike damage accumulation creates attrition pressure
environmental_storytelling: California highway settings suggest illegal street racing culture
Procedural obstacles (traffic, animals) frame racing as lawless/dangerous
Repair shops and police mechanics imply underground racing economy
Multi-level progression structure implies growing infamy/difficulty
